text.title: hybrid-search-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for hybrid-search-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using equal weights (alpha=0.5) for all query types without per-domain tuning.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A mistuned alpha underperforms pure vector search on semantic-heavy queries and pure BM25 on keyword-heavy queries. Equal weighting is the starting point, not the optimal value for most domains.

failure-mode.description: Dynamic alpha must be computed before the retrieval call; an agent that decides alpha post-retrieval cannot change the result.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Alpha affects which documents are retrieved. Deciding after retrieval cannot retroactively change the result set.

failure-mode.description: BM25 scores are unbounded and distribution-dependent — never compare raw BM25 scores across different corpora or index configurations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: BM25 scores depend on corpus statistics. Comparing raw scores across corpora is meaningless.

failure-mode.description: When using EnsembleRetriever in LangChain, set weights explicitly; the default equal weighting is rarely optimal.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Equal weighting is the starting point but almost never the optimal configuration for production.

failure-mode.description: Qdrant's sparse vector format requires SPLADE or BM25 pre-computed sparse encodings — you cannot pass raw text; tokenize and encode first.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Qdrant sparse vectors are pre-computed representations, not raw text that the database tokenizes.

failure-mode.description: Human-in-loop checkpoint: when a query returns 0 results from both retrievers, escalate to a query-expansion step or human clarification rather than returning an empty answer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Zero-result queries indicate a mismatch between query and corpus. Graceful degradation with query expansion is better than empty results.
